meta.market_tone: Flat benchmarks; weak breadth and cautious risk appetite

domestic: ICICI Lombard plunged after weak quarterly earnings
FIIs sold heavily while DIIs absorbed part of the supply
Weekly derivatives expiry increased intraday volatility
global: Brent crude remained near 85 dollars per barrel
Benign U.S. inflation reduced near-term Fed hike expectations

deep_dive.full_text: Confirmed facts: Nifty slipped 0.02% to 24,072.75 and Sensex closed flat at 77,186.87. Nine of sixteen sectors advanced, but midcaps and smallcaps fell 0.4% and 0.1%. IT gained 0.7%, while financial services lost 0.5%. ICICI Lombard fell 10.5% after weak results and higher claims provisions; Dixon rose 6.3% following cabinet support for electronics manufacturing. Provisional data showed FII selling of Rs 4,205.56 crore and DII buying of Rs 2,986.41 crore. The rupee weakened to 96.345 per dollar. Globally, U.S.-Iran tensions kept Brent near 85 dollars, Asian chip shares sold off, and the U.S. 10-year yield was near 4.58%. Reasoned interpretation: index stability depended on DII support and IT gains, but weaker breadth, heavy FII selling, currency pressure and oil risk leave the near-term setup fragile. Earnings guidance and Middle East developments are likely to determine the next directional move.
deep_dive.summary_takeaway: DII support and IT strength held the indices flat, but heavy FII selling, weak breadth, rupee pressure and elevated crude keep near-term risk tilted slightly negative.

outlook.base_case: Range-bound to mildly negative, with earnings and geopolitical headlines driving stock-specific volatility
key_watch: Wipro and Tech Mahindra earnings and guidance
Brent crude and Strait of Hormuz developments
